{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Management Director",
  "description": "Manufacturer of aluminum building components. The company is engaged in manufacturing and marketing building products including aluminium doors, windows, railings and suspended ceilings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Management Director

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Account Management Director to lead and optimize our Account Management team.
	This leadership role will focus on driving customer success and revenue growth, ensuring long-term customer retention, and enhancing overall account health.
	The ideal candidate will possess a track record of exceeding expansion and retention targets within a fast-paced startup or SaaS company.


Responsibilities


	Lead the Account Management team with a customer-centric approach, prioritizing customer success and growth
	Develop and execute proactive customer retention and expansion strategies
	Analyze customer experience and health metrics, identifying key indicators and trends
	Educate and influence peers and the team on marketplace dynamics and successful strategies
	Contribute to the development of new product and service offerings based on customer needs
	Maximize the effectiveness of Account Management tools and resources
	Act as an escalation point for complex negotiations, optimizing deal structures
	Promote a positive, diverse, and inclusive corporate culture aligned with company values.


Requirements and skills


	7+ years of Customer Success/Account Management Leadership experience in a fast-paced startup or SaaS company
	Proven success in exceeding expansion and retention targets
	Strong leadership capability in building and managing high-performing teams
	Experience with negotiating diverse customer accounts, from SMB to enterprise
	Ability to develop strategic plans and optimize processes for long-term scalability
	Empathy for customers combined with a passion for revenue growth
	Excellent collaboration skills with cross-functional teams
	Highly organized with strong written and verbal communication abilities.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: Globalaluminiumindustries@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Account Management Director Job at Manufacturer of Aluminum Building Components




        
    

        
        Manufacturer of aluminum building components. The company is engaged in manufacturing and marketing building products including aluminium doors, windows, railings and suspended ceilings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Management Director

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced Account Management Director to lead and optimize our Account Management team.
	This leadership role will focus on driving customer success and revenue growth, ensuring long-term customer retention, and enhancing overall account health.
	The ideal candidate will possess a track record of exceeding expansion and retention targets within a fast-paced startup or SaaS company.


Responsibilities


	Lead the Account Management team with a customer-centric approach, prioritizing customer success and growth
	Develop and execute proactive customer retention and expansion strategies
	Analyze customer experience and health metrics, identifying key indicators and trends
	Educate and influence peers and the team on marketplace dynamics and successful strategies
	Contribute to the development of new product and service offerings based on customer needs
	Maximize the effectiveness of Account Management tools and resources
	Act as an escalation point for complex negotiations, optimizing deal structures
	Promote a positive, diverse, and inclusive corporate culture aligned with company values.


Requirements and skills


	7+ years of Customer Success/Account Management Leadership experience in a fast-paced startup or SaaS company
	Proven success in exceeding expansion and retention targets
	Strong leadership capability in building and managing high-performing teams
	Experience with negotiating diverse customer accounts, from SMB to enterprise
	Ability to develop strategic plans and optimize processes for long-term scalability
	Empathy for customers combined with a passion for revenue growth
	Excellent collaboration skills with cross-functional teams
	Highly organized with strong written and verbal communication abilities.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

title: Job Openings at Proforce Galaxies Limited
content:
Proforce Galaxies is a forward-looking advanced space technology and defense-focused company committed to delivering advanced satellite and space-based solutions that support national security, strategic intelligence, and critical infrastructure protection. Our capabilities are centered on the design, development, and deployment of high-performance systems that enable secure communications, real-time surveillance, and data-driven decision-making. At Proforce Galaxies, we are dedicated to strengthening technological sovereignty, enhancing situational awareness, and advancing aerospace innovation to support mission-critical operations and long-term national and global security objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



1.) Communication Engineer

Job Location: Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 


	Are you passionate about advanced communication systems and eager to work on groundbreaking satellite and aerospace technologies?
	Proforce Galaxies Limited, an innovative company dedicated to becoming a leader in Africa’s space and satellite industry, is seeking a talented Communication Engineer to join our growing technical team.


Key Responsibilities


	Design, develop, and implement communication systems for space-based and terrestrial platforms.
	Configure and test RF, satellite, and wireless communication equipment.
	Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams on integration of communication subsystems into aerospace platforms.
	Perform link budget analysis, signal processing, and protocol optimization.
	Monitor and troubleshoot network performance and signal integrity.
	Ensure compliance with relevant regulatory, safety, and quality standards.
	Research and propose new communication technologies and architectures to support innovation.


Qualifications & Experience


	B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Telecommunications, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of hands-on experience in communication system design or RF engineering.
	Solid understanding of satellite communication, RF circuits, and digital modulation techniques.
	Proficiency in tools such as MATLAB, HFSS, CST, or similar for signal and antenna simulations.
	Experience with software-defined radio (SDR), IoT communication protocols, or 5G is an advantage.


Preferred Skills:


	Knowledge of satellite link design and VSAT systems.
	Hands-on experience with hardware integration and testing.
	Familiarity with low-earth orbit (LEO) satellite networks.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.


What We Offer


	Salary Range: N300,000 - N400,000 per month.
	Opportunity to work on cutting-edge communication technologies in the space-tech sector.
	Inclusive, innovative, and mission-driven work environment.
	Career advancement in a future-facing organization.


Application Closing Date: 20th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested candidates should send their CV, portfolio or links to past projects and Proof of hands-on experience to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Business Development Manager (BDM)

Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Space Technology | Geospatial Intelligence | AI & Software Solutions

Position Overview


	We are seeking a strategic and dynamic Business Development Manager (BDM) to drive sales and expand market presence for Proforce Galaxies’ advanced technological solutions across Nigeria, Africa, and global markets.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of high-tech systems, particularly in AI, geospatial intelligence, defense technology, and satellite systems, and will play a pivotal role in growing our client base, managing partnerships, and leading all commercial efforts to achieve measurable sales growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales of the company’s full product portfolio - including Satellites, Ground Stations, Satellite Data Products - to both private and public sector clients.
	Represent the company at exhibitions, conferences, and technology expos, articulating product features and advantages with expert precision.
	Develop and execute comprehensive sales and business growth strategies aimed at achieving a minimum of 100% sales increase within defined periods.
	Identify, engage, and close business opportunities with government agencies, multinational organizations, research institutions, and private enterprises.
	Maintain and nurture strong relationships with clients, partners, and stakeholders, ensuring long-term business engagement and satisfaction.
	Collaborate closely with internal teams including software engineers, GIS analysts, and AI developers to tailor solutions that meet client-specific needs.
	Gather and report market intelligence and customer feedback to support product improvement and innovation.
	Prepare detailed business proposals, sales forecasts, and strategic reports for management review.
	Promote Proforce Galaxies as a global thought leader in space and geospatial intelligence solutions.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree (Master’s preferred) in Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering, or related technical discipline.
	5–7 years of experience in Business Development, Technical Sales, or Strategic Partnerships within Technology, Defense, or Engineering sectors.
	Proven track record of achieving or exceeding sales targets, especially for high-value or B2B technology solutions.
	Strong understanding of AI, GIS, Space Systems, and emerging technologies.
	Exceptional communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
	Ability to work cross-functionally with technical teams and translate complex products into clear business value.
	Willingness to travel locally and internationally for exhibitions, client meetings, and industry events.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 per month based on experience.

Why Join Proforce Galaxies?


	Work with a forward-thinking organization driving Africa’s leadership in space and geospatial technology.
	Be part of a multidisciplinary, world-class team of engineers, data scientists, and innovators.
	Competitive compensation and performance-based incentives.
	Global exposure and opportunity to represent cutting-edge African innovation on the international stage.


Application Closing Date: 15th May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter (highlighting relevant achievements and experience) to: [email protected] using "Application Business Development Manager (BDM)" the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

 

3.) Mobile App Developer

Job Location: Ode-Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time (On-site)
Sector: Space and Satellite Technology

Role Overview


	We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Mobile App Developer to join our growing team of engineers and technology professionals.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing and building intuitive, scalable, and high-performance mobile applications that provide users with seamless access to real-time satellite data and AI-powered insights.


Key Responsibilities


	Mobile Application Development: Design, develop, and maintain cross-platform mobile applications (Android & iOS) using React Native.
	API Integration: Collaborate with Software team to integrate RESTful APIs, cloud services, and real-time geospatial data.
	Data Visualization: Work closely with GIS analysts and AI engineers to develop interactive dashboards, maps, and visualization tools.
	Performance Optimization: Ensure applications are secure, responsive, and optimized for performance, scalability, and offline functionality where required.
	Cross-Platform Consistency: Deliver a consistent and high-quality user experience across multiple devices and platforms.
	Testing & Debugging: Perform unit, integration, and functional testing to ensure application reliability and stability.
	Collaboration: Engage with cross-functional teams (frontend, backend, AI) to deliver integrated solutions.
	Continuous Improvement: Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in mobile development.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree (B.Sc.) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in mobile application development or a similar role.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (mandatory requirement).
	Solid understanding of REST APIs, JSON, and third-party integrations.
	Experience deploying applications to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store.
	Familiarity with geospatial tools and mapping technologies (e.g., Mapbox, Leaflet, Google Maps API) is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of mobile security best practices and offline-first architecture.
	Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is desirable.


What We Offer


	Salary: N250,000 - N350,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to contribute to Africa’s rapidly evolving space-tech ecosystem.
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovation-driven work environment.
	Exposure to high-impact, globally relevant projects.
	Continuous professional development and growth opportunities.
	Competitive compensation and benefits package.


Application Closing Date: 31st May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV), Portfolio or links to completed projects, or Evidence of hands-on experience to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Secretary Job at CFL Group




        
    

        
        CFL Group was established in 1998 with the founding of NEXT TECHNOLOGY NIGERIA LIMITED, a telecommunications company that provided internet services to a growing customer base. The company was founded by Engr. Lai Omotola. Over the next nine years, CFL Group expanded significantly, incorporating an additional 14 companies to form the diversified conglomerate known today as CFL Group.

In 2015, CFL Group transitioned into a full-fledged infrastructure company with interests spanning Real Estate, Energy, Power, Construction, Agriculture, and Transportation. As a wholly indigenous Nigerian company headquartered in Lagos, CFL Group is committed to contributing to the nation’s development across multiple sectors.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting a smart, organized, and experienced candidate to join our team in the capacity below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Minimum of 5 years’ experience as a secretary or administrative officer
	Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
	Excellent communication and organizational skills
	Ability to manage office records, correspondence, and schedules efficiently
	Good typing and reporting skills.


Qualification:


	OND/HND/Bachelor’s Degree in any related field.


Location:


	Mainland, Lagos Ketu, Ojota, Ogudu and Oworo preferable.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using “Secretary Application” as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Support Engineer",
  "description": "The Concept Group is a holding company for companies established in 1992. Subsidiaries under the Group include: Rosabon Financial Services - Nigeria&rsquo;s Leading Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing Company, Concept Nova - Bespoke Enterprise IT Solutions Company,Percy Aitkins - Bureau De Change.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Engineer
Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are a dynamic organization seeking a competent and proactive IT Support Engineer to join our team and support internal IT operations.
	The IT Support Engineer will be responsible for providing technical support to internal users, maintaining IT systems, and ensuring efficient operation of hardware and infrastructure.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide first and second-level technical support to users
	Troubleshoot hardware and software issues
	Log, track, and resolve IT support tickets
	Support desktops, laptops, printers, telephones, and mobile devices
	Manage IT asset inventory and conduct regular audits
	Configure and deploy new systems and devices
	Support Active Directory and access control systems
	Document troubleshooting procedures and IT processes


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Achieve 90% ticket resolution within SLA
	Respond to support requests within 30 minutes
	Maintain 100% accuracy in asset inventory
	Ensure high user satisfaction (minimum 4.5 rating)


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
	Relevant certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Microsoft Certified, ITIL)
	Proven experience in IT support, system administration, or networking
	Good knowledge of current IT tools and systems
	Ability to provide 1st and 2nd level support


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: tonna.nwobodo@conceptgroup-ng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

IT Support Engineer Job at the Concept Group




        
    

        
        The Concept Group is a holding company for companies established in 1992. Subsidiaries under the Group include: Rosabon Financial Services - Nigeria’s Leading Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing Company, Concept Nova - Bespoke Enterprise IT Solutions Company,Percy Aitkins - Bureau De Change.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Engineer
Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are a dynamic organization seeking a competent and proactive IT Support Engineer to join our team and support internal IT operations.
	The IT Support Engineer will be responsible for providing technical support to internal users, maintaining IT systems, and ensuring efficient operation of hardware and infrastructure.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide first and second-level technical support to users
	Troubleshoot hardware and software issues
	Log, track, and resolve IT support tickets
	Support desktops, laptops, printers, telephones, and mobile devices
	Manage IT asset inventory and conduct regular audits
	Configure and deploy new systems and devices
	Support Active Directory and access control systems
	Document troubleshooting procedures and IT processes


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Achieve 90% ticket resolution within SLA
	Respond to support requests within 30 minutes
	Maintain 100% accuracy in asset inventory
	Ensure high user satisfaction (minimum 4.5 rating)


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
	Relevant certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Microsoft Certified, ITIL)
	Proven experience in IT support, system administration, or networking
	Good knowledge of current IT tools and systems
	Ability to provide 1st and 2nd level support


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Healthcare Assistant",
  "description": "Castville Solutions is a recruitment and business solutions provider specializing in the healthcare sector. We focus on connecting healthcare facilities with highly skilled professionals, enabling efficient operations, and driving business growth. Our services extend to recruitment, Templates for process and operations management, SOP development, and healthcare-specific training, with a clear mission to transform the healthcare workforce and operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Healthcare Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	Join our growing pharmacy team!
	We are looking for a friendly, organized, and compassionate Healthcare Assistant to support daily pharmacy operations and provide excellent customer care.


Responsibilities


	Assist customers with inquiries and prescriptions
	Support pharmacists with daily tasks
	Manage stock and inventory
	Maintain the cleanliness and organization of the pharmacy
	Handle customer records and basic administrative duties


Requirements


	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic knowledge of healthcare or pharmacy operations is an advantage
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
	Computer literacy and attention to detail
	Previous experience is a plus but not mandatory.


Salary
N60,000 - N70,000 per month.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-10",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Castville Solutions"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Healthcare Assistant Job at Castville Solutions




        
    

        
        Castville Solutions is a recruitment and business solutions provider specializing in the healthcare sector. We focus on connecting healthcare facilities with highly skilled professionals, enabling efficient operations, and driving business growth. Our services extend to recruitment, Templates for process and operations management, SOP development, and healthcare-specific training, with a clear mission to transform the healthcare workforce and operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Healthcare Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Join our growing pharmacy team!
	We are looking for a friendly, organized, and compassionate Healthcare Assistant to support daily pharmacy operations and provide excellent customer care.


Responsibilities


	Assist customers with inquiries and prescriptions
	Support pharmacists with daily tasks
	Manage stock and inventory
	Maintain the cleanliness and organization of the pharmacy
	Handle customer records and basic administrative duties


Requirements


	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic knowledge of healthcare or pharmacy operations is an advantage
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
	Computer literacy and attention to detail
	Previous experience is a plus but not mandatory.


Salary
N60,000 - N70,000 per month.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
